_note: EXAMPLE ONLY — a generic 3-tier web app so `node gen-diagram.mjs` runs out of the box. Replace this entire file with one generated from YOUR real codebase per SKILL.md. No mock data in production: every node/edge must be verified against real source.
summary: Example: a generic web application — browser SPA → REST API → Postgres + Redis, with a worker and an object store. Replace with your real, code-verified system.

groups.label: Edge / Ingress
groups.blurb: Public-facing entrypoints — CDN, load balancer, API gateway.

groups.label: Application
groups.blurb: Backend services, workers, and business logic.

groups.label: Datastores
groups.blurb: Primary DB, cache, and object storage.

groups.label: Clients
groups.blurb: Browsers, mobile apps, and SDKs that call the API.

nodes.label: CDN / LB
nodes.detail: Terminates TLS and routes traffic to the API and static assets.

nodes.label: REST API
nodes.detail: Public REST API serving the SPA and mobile clients.

nodes.label: Background Worker
nodes.detail: Processes async jobs off the queue (email, exports, webhooks).

nodes.label: Auth Service
nodes.detail: Issues and validates credentials for the API.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL
nodes.detail: System of record for users and domain entities.

nodes.label: Redis
nodes.detail: Session cache, rate-limit counters, and the job queue.

nodes.label: Web App (SPA)
nodes.detail: Browser dashboard that calls the REST API.

nodes.label: Client SDK
nodes.detail: Typed client published for third-party integrations.

productionReadiness.why: SDK is beta and not yet published to the registry.
productionReadiness.nextStep: Publish v1.0 and add CI release.
